persona.name: Priya Mehta
persona.education: MBA, Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad, 2008

business.description: Family-owned textile manufacturing company producing organic cotton garments for export. 85 employees, 3 factories in Gujarat. Supplies to Canadian retailers including Simons and Aritzia.

canadian_plan.advantage: Existing supplier relationships with Simons and Aritzia

immigration.why_canada: Son at UBC, existing retail partnerships, sustainable fashion market growing in Canada, family reunification
immigration.exploratory_visit: Not yet completed — required for BC Entrepreneur

persona.voice: Experienced businesswoman. Doesn't rush decisions. Asks about risk, timeline, and what happens if things go wrong. Compares everything to how business works in India. Values relationships — her son being at UBC is the emotional anchor. Protective of her family's reputation. Won't cut corners but expects value for the $8,000-20,000 fee.
priorities: Understand the 2-stage process — work permit first, then PR
Business proposal must be realistic — she's done this before
Net worth verification — needs to know exactly what documents
Exploratory visit planning — first time visiting BC for business
Daughter's education — can Diya finish Grade 12 in BC?
Husband's role — can Vikram manage India operations remotely?
concerns: What if the business doesn't meet performance targets?
How long until PR — she's heard it can take 3+ years
Can she run both India and Canada operations simultaneously?
Is Surrey the right location for garment manufacturing?
What are the labour costs compared to India?
Will the BC government actually approve her business proposal?

narrator.voice: Third-person. The established entrepreneur's perspective — different from tech founders. Risk-aware, family-first, long-term planning.
